I INTRODUCTION 
 
I.1 Product name  
GLOBAL_OMI_HEALTH_carbon_ph_area_averaged 
 
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
The OMI yearly mean surface sea water pH reported on total hydrogen ion scale is computed 
from monthly pH values which are distributed as part of the CMEMS product 
MULTIOBS_GLO_BIO_CARBON_SURFACE_REP_015_008.  
At each grid cell and monthly resolution,  the speciation software CO2sys (Lewis and Wallace, 
1998; Van Heuven et al., 2011)  is applied to obtain  the best estimate of pH (µij,m). Monthly 
uncertainty per cell (σij,m) is evaluated from the contributions of (1) model randomness, (2) 
systematic errors. The uncertainty of (1) is derived from the standard deviation of 100 ensemble 
members of the reconstructed spco2 (Chau et al. 2022). Systematic uncertainty (2) is evaluated 
through uncertainty propagation following Orr et al. (2018) within the speciation software 
CO2sys. Inputs to the CO2sys uncertainty propagation routine are default values for dissociation 
constants, systematic errors, ensemble-based uncertainty estimates of spco2, and analysis 
errors from temperature, salinity, and nutrients (see Chau et al, 2023 for further details). Taking 
these propagated uncertainties results in standard uncertainties ( σij,m) of monthly pH fields. 
The OMI corresponds to the yearly mean of global area-weighted monthly mean pH values (µyr) 
and total uncertainty (σyr). The two quanties are computed as follows 
µyr  =  (Σm Σij µ ij,m ×Aij) / (12 × ΣijAij);  σyr2  =  (Σm Σij σij,m2 ×Aij) / (12 × ΣijAij) 
where (µij,m; σij,m) are the best estimate and uncertainty of CO2sys pH at the unit of month and 
grid cell and Aij is the cell area.
